evals.prompt: Write a handbook for the context-injection system at /Users/jp/Projects/active/claude-code-tool-dev/packages/plugins/cross-model/context-injection/. This is the vendored MCP server within the cross-model plugin. Explore the system thoroughly, then write a complete operational handbook covering bring-up, component runbooks, failure modes, and verification.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive HANDBOOK.md covering all major components, entrypoints (execute_scout, process_turn MCP tools), configuration, failure modes, and a verification procedure.

assertions.name: handbook_file_exists
assertions.description: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 1000 characters of content

assertions.name: has_overview_section
assertions.description: The handbook has an Overview or introduction section that describes the system's purpose

assertions.name: covers_both_mcp_tools
assertions.description: The handbook mentions both execute_scout and process_turn (the two main MCP tool entrypoints)

assertions.name: has_failure_modes_table
assertions.description: The handbook contains at least one table with symptom/cause/recovery columns (or equivalent failure mode documentation)

assertions.name: has_verification_section
assertions.description: The handbook includes a numbered verification or smoke test procedure an operator can actually run

evals.prompt: Audit the HANDBOOK.md at /Users/jp/Projects/active/claude-code-tool-dev/packages/plugins/cross-model/HANDBOOK.md. Check for accuracy issues, coverage gaps, currency concerns, and structural problems against the actual source code. Recent commits include: approval-policy default changed from on-failure to on-request, JSON epilogue made the sole machine contract for analytics, credential scan gaps closed, certifi CA bundle exempted from secret-file gate.
evals.expected_output: An audit report with sections for Accuracy Issues, Coverage Gaps, Currency Concerns, Structural Issues, and Recommended Priority. Report only — do not edit the handbook.

assertions.name: report_file_exists
assertions.description: audit_report.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 500 characters of content

assertions.name: has_four_audit_categories
assertions.description: Report contains all four standard categories: Accuracy Issues, Coverage Gaps, Currency Concerns, and Structural Issues (or equivalent headings)

assertions.name: flags_approval_policy_drift
assertions.description: Report explicitly identifies the approval-policy default change (on-failure to on-request) as an accuracy issue or currency concern

assertions.name: no_edits_to_source
assertions.description: The report is findings-only — it describes issues rather than applying fixes. The report does not claim to have modified the handbook.

assertions.name: has_priority_ranking
assertions.description: Report ends with or contains a Recommended Priority or priority ranking section

evals.prompt: The approval-policy default changed from on-failure to on-request in the cross-model plugin (commit fa1531b). Update the handbook at /Users/jp/Projects/active/claude-code-tool-dev/packages/plugins/cross-model/HANDBOOK.md to reflect this change. Find every section that references the approval-policy default and update them accurately.
evals.expected_output: The relevant handbook sections updated to show on-request as the default approval policy, with on-failure removed or marked as the old default. Only affected sections changed; no rewriting of unrelated content.

assertions.name: output_file_exists
assertions.description: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has substantial content (at least 5000 characters)

assertions.name: new_default_present
assertions.description: The output HANDBOOK.md contains 'on-request' as the approval-policy default value

assertions.name: old_default_not_current
assertions.description: The output HANDBOOK.md does not present 'on-failure' as the current/default approval-policy value (historical references OK, but not as the operative default)

assertions.name: structure_preserved
assertions.description: Major section headings from the original handbook are preserved — targeted update, not a full rewrite. At least 80% of original headings present.
